module.title: Week 35: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules & Dashboards

topics.title: Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Develop correlation rules
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules and its role in SIEM rules, dashboards, query languages
topics.interviewQuestion: What are correlation rules in SIEM?
topics.interviewAnswer: Correlation rules link multiple seemingly unrelated events across different log sources to detect multi-step attack chains. Without correlation rules, analysts would drown in isolated alerts. Modern SIEM platforms like Splunk, Elastic Security, Microsoft Sentinel, and QRadar each have their own correlation language.

quiz.question: What do correlation rules link together?
Which SIEM platform uses KQL?
What does a ransomware correlation pattern include?
options: PowerShell + mass file rename + ransom note

quiz.question: What happens with too short time windows?
What happens with too long time windows?
Which best describes Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Advanced SIEM Correlation Rules can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: SIEM Dashboard Design
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: SIEM Dashboard Design is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Design effective dashboards
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 2: SIEM Dashboard Design and its role in SIEM rules, dashboards, query languages
topics.interviewQuestion: What makes an effective SOC dashboard?
topics.interviewAnswer: Effective SOC dashboards follow the three-second rule - an analyst should grasp the current security state within three seconds. Top section for critical metrics: active incidents by severity, MTTD, MTTR. Middle sections for trending data: top attack vectors, affected assets. Choose KPIs that drive action.

topics.title: Day 3: Advanced Query Languages
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Advanced Query Languages is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Master SIEM query languages
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 3: Advanced Query Languages and its role in SIEM rules, dashboards, query languages
topics.interviewQuestion: What advanced features do SIEM query languages offer?
topics.interviewAnswer: Splunk SPL: transaction, eventstats, predict, subsearch, lookup. Elastic Security EQL: sequence-based detections, joins. Sentinel KQL: let statements, join operations, time series. Mastering these enables complex correlations and statistical analysis.

quiz.question: What SPL command groups related events?
What EQL feature enables sequence detection?
What does eventstats do?
What enriches events with external intel?
What does predict do?
Which best describes Day 3: Advanced Query Languages?

topics.title: Day 4: SIEM Performance Optimization
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: SIEM Performance Optimization is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Optimize SIEM performance
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 4: SIEM Performance Optimization and its role in SIEM rules, dashboards, query languages
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you optimize SIEM performance?
topics.interviewAnswer: Index management: proper data typing, field extraction at index time vs search time. Query optimization: use earliest/latest time bounds, avoid leading wildcards, use summary indexing for frequent queries. Hardware sizing: CPU for correlation, RAM for search, SSD for hot storage.

quiz.question: What is faster for field extraction?
What query practice degrades performance?
What storage type is for hot data?
What CPU aspect matters most?
What reduces repeat computation?
Which best describes Day 4: SIEM Performance Optimization?
quiz.explanation: Day 4: SIEM Performance Optimization is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 5: SIEM Use Case Development
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: SIEM Use Case Development is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write SIEM use cases
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 5: SIEM Use Case Development and its role in SIEM rules, dashboards, query languages
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the SIEM use case development lifecycle?
topics.interviewAnswer: Identify detection gap. Research attack technique (MITRE ATT&CK). Write detection logic. Create test dataset. Validate true positives. Tune false positives. Document use case. Deploy to production. Monitor and refine. Each use case should map to a specific ATT&CK technique ID.

quiz.question: What is first step in use case development?
What framework maps detection techniques?
What follows writing detection logic?
What is the tuning phase focused on?
options: Reduce false positives while maintaining detection

quiz.question: What should each use case map to?
Which best describes Day 5: SIEM Use Case Development?
quiz.explanation: Day 5: SIEM Use Case Development is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.
